David Ohm, PhD 

☰
    • About
    • Blog
    • Papers
  		1.

    Paper introducing a method for using deep neural networks (DNN) to perform RF emitter localization.

    Trevor Landeen, Jake Gunther, Todd Moon, David Ohm, Robert North. Association of Emitter and Emission Using Deep Learning. 2017 51st Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers, 2011.

    URL  

  2. Paper introducing RF radio design course and teaching concepts.

    Jake Gunther, David Ohm, Todd Moon. Filling the Curriculum Gap - A Course in RF to Baseband Systems Engineering for Radio Receiver Design. DSP/SPE Meeting 2011, 2011.

    URL  

  3. Paper on new 3D correlation function for estimating TDOA, FDOA and Cyclic Frequency

    David Ohm. Emitter location via joint 3-D parametric estimation. Asilomar Signals and Systems Conference, 2009.

    URL  

  4. Using kinematic and cyclostationary parameter estimation to geolocate signals in a co-channel interference environment

    David Ohm. Kinematic and cyclostationary parameter estimation for co-channel emitter location applications. PhD Dissertation, 2007.

    URL   PDF  

  5. Paper introducing an algorithm for extracting acoustic micro-signatures from sensor data.

    David Ohm. Acoustic Microsignatures - New Extraction Concepts. Asilomar Signals and Systems Conference, 2005.

    URL  

  6. Paper demonstrating the method for fast correlation called overlap-scrap.

    David Ohm. Fast Correlation Processing. Internal report, 2005.

    URL   PDF  

  7. Use of classical and modern two-dimensional spectral estimation techniques for enhancing inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) imagery.

    David Ohm. Enhanced Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar Imagery Using 2-D Spectral Estimation. Master's Thesis, 2004.

    URL   PDF
